Container.Name: LeakWatch
Container.Overview:
LeakWatch teaches you which of your Docker containers should be behind a VPN - and shows you, at a glance, whether they actually are.
Building an *arr stack and not sure what needs a VPN? LeakWatch lists every container, tells you whether a VPN is REQUIRED (torrent/usenet), RECOMMENDED (indexers, *arr apps) or NOT NEEDED (Plex, dashboards, databases), and explains WHY in plain language. Then it checks each container's real public IP and flags anything that should be protected but is leaking your server's real IP.
For new users it is a guide to safe setup; for experienced users it is a single dashboard to confirm everything is configured as expected and to catch a VPN that silently dropped.
How it checks: a throwaway "sidecar" container runs inside each target's network namespace and reports the real exit IP (works even if the app has no curl/wget), cross-checked across several IP services. It best-effort names the VPN provider (Mullvad, NordVPN, Surfshark, Proton, PIA and more), detects tun/wg tunnels, recognises Tailscale/ZeroTier mesh networks, and includes an IPv6-leak and Tor-exit check.
Requires the Docker socket to be mounted (read/write) so it can list and probe your containers. The scan runs automatically when you open the page.
Container.Description: Teaches which containers should be behind a VPN and why, then checks each one's real public IP and flags anything that should be protected but is leaking your server's real IP. Great for setting up and auditing an *arr stack.
Container.Changes:
### 3.3.0 - Accurate VPN-provider naming using a proxy-detection database (names Surfshark, Mullvad, NordVPN, Proton, PIA, and more).
### 3.2.0 - Scan now runs automatically on page load; version shown in the footer.
### 3.1.0 - Per-app recommendations (VPN required / recommended / not needed) with plain-language "why" on hover and a full "About this app" panel. - Per-container scan checklist on hover; Tailscale/ZeroTier mesh awareness; IPv6-leak and Tor-exit checks.
### 1.0.0 - Initial release: list containers, find each exit IP, compare to the host, flag leaks.

Config.Name: WebUI Port
Config.Description: Port for the LeakWatch web interface.

Config.Name: Docker Socket
Config.Description: Required. Lets LeakWatch list your containers and run network probes. Grants Docker control - normal for this kind of tool.

Config.Name: Helper Image
Config.Description: Tiny image used to probe each container's network namespace.

Config.Name: Scan Concurrency
Config.Description: How many containers to test in parallel.

Config.Name: proxycheck.io API Key (optional)
Config.Description: Optional free proxycheck.io key for sharper VPN-provider naming (1000 lookups/day). Works without a key at a lower limit.
